content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 607 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

November 3, 1976

Mr. Richard Goldman
10880 Wilshire Boulevard, 19th Floor
Los Angeles, CA 90024

Dear Mr. Goldman:

This letter is to confirm the advice I gave you over the telephone on November 1. The facts as I understand them are as follows.

You represent a police officers' association which sponsors a political action committee. In order to raise funds for its treasury, the association conducted a fund raising event. Subsequently, the PAC applied to the association for a large initial contribution, which after deliberation by the association's board of directors was, at least in part, granted. The association has no other political activities. The PAC has fully reported the association as the source of its operating revenues in the two pre-election reports which it has filed. In light of these facts, you asked whether the association, as well as the PAC, had a filing obligation.

Currently, the Commission is considering a similar problem in the opinion requested by Ruth Hayes (No. 76-069). Thus, the following circumstances exist with respect to your question.

1. The Commission has not ultimately determined the answer to this question;

2. The PAC fully disclosed the contribution on its statement; and

3. The association's statement would not be filed until after the election.

Thus, under these circumstances, I advised you that the association need not file a campaign statement unless and

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5601 • ENFORCEMENT COMPLEX (same number

[119]

Mr. Richard Goldman
Page Two
November 3, 1976


until the Hayes opinion determined that a filing is required
in such situations. Obviously, any delay in filing occurring
because of adherence to this advice could not be the subject
of a late fine.

     If you have any further questions, please feel free
to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Ted Prim
                                        Staff Counsel
                                        Legal Division

TP:jo

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a political action committee (PAC) formed by a police officers' association to raise funds must disclose its funding sources and whether the association has an obligation to report contributions received from the PAC.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that the PAC must fully disclose its contributions on its campaign statement, and the association need not report PAC contributions as personal income unless specific circumstances are met. The Commission has not yet made a final determination on certain aspects of the question.

Large portions of text are corrupted or unreadable, making full extraction difficult. No clear QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections visible due to formatting degradation. Question and conclusion synthesized from partially legible content. Recipient name appears corrupted (Goldman vs. Go). Related to Commission request No. 76-069 by Ruth Hayes.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advises that the PAC must fully disclose its contributions on its campaign statement, and the association need not report PAC contributions as personal income unless specific circumstances are met. The Commission has not yet made a final determination on certain aspects of the question.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to an inquiry about disclosure obligations for a police officers' association PAC and the association's reporting requirements for PAC contributions. The letter confirms telephonic advice given on November 1, 1976, and provides guidance on proper disclosure procedures.
